assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can receive email when the selected route requires it.
No platform account, tenant, credentials, or product resources exist yet.
Developer has an active cloud provider account (AWS, GCP, or Azure) with admin or owner permissions
Developer has a deployable application with a start command or Dockerfile, pushed to GitHub or container registry

developer_goal: Deploy their first application on Porter

first_success.outcome: Developer successfully deploys their application and accesses it live on a public URL
first_success.observable_signal: Application is live and accessible at *.onporter.run URL shown in the Porter dashboard

route_selection.rule: Reconstruct complete official candidate routes, prefer no payment/admin/approval when possible, then minimize required actions; leave consequential ties unresolved.

candidates.name: CLI-based bundle installation
candidates.reason: Present in the machine draft; not selected by an independent shortest-path audit.

candidates.name: Porter CLI
candidates.reason: CLI is used mainly for bundle installation and management after initial setup; the first-mile onboarding focuses on the web dashboard for project creation and app deployment.

prerequisites.description: Active cloud provider account (AWS, GCP, or Azure) with admin or owner permissions to provision infrastructure
Deployable application with a start command or Dockerfile, pushed to a GitHub repository or container registry

external_gates.description: Creating a Porter account and project requires valid project name and successful account creation
Cloud provider account must have admin or owner permissions to create Kubernetes clusters and IAM roles
For AWS, user must acknowledge IAM resource creation in CloudFormation and create the stack
User must review and accept estimated monthly costs for infrastructure provisioning
Connecting GitHub requires authorizing the Porter GitHub App and selecting repository access
User must merge the GitHub Actions PR to trigger deployment

platform_outcomes.description: Application is deployed and accessible via a public URL on Porter

sources.title: Quickstart
evidence_supported: Step 1: Sign up and create a project

uncertainties.question: What are the detailed cluster setup steps and requirements for GCP and Azure cloud providers?
uncertainties.impact: Potential differences in friction gates, permissions, and provisioning steps could affect onboarding experience and time to first success.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: The official quickstart documentation details AWS cluster setup extensively but only mentions GCP and Azure without detailed steps.

uncertainties.question: Is there an officially documented time estimate for time to first success from account creation to live application?
uncertainties.impact: Knowing exact time expectations helps set user expectations and measure onboarding efficiency.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: The documentation states 'Deploy your first application to Porter in minutes' but does not provide a precise or vendor-claimed time.

uncertainties.question: Has the machine-drafted Porter route been independently shortest-path audited?
uncertainties.impact: Action counts and peer comparison must stay withheld until a human audit passes.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Complete a shortest-path audit against current official docs and mark verified only when evidence supports it.
